assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies the issue as cert-manager webhook validation failures causing pod creation problems

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and mentions checking mutatingwebhookconfigurations

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to Ignore and kubectl delete command as alternative

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' as alternative emergency fix

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Notes that deleting webhook allows pods to create normally and emphasizes it's temporary until proper reinstall

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check cert-manager version and references compatibility documentation

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response mentions upgrading to support Kubernetes 1.32 (not 1.30), but the expectation specifically asks for 1.30 support

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Primary solution uses kubectl patch to set failurePolicy to Ignore, presented as less drastic than deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl run test-pod' command to immediately test pod creation after applying the fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention persisting changes in source of truth like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Clearly presents failurePolicy: Ignore as temporary emergency mitigation and includes step to restore original Fail policy
